evals.prompt: I have results for a paper: our small retrieval model beats a larger baseline on biomedical QA when the query contains rare terminology, but loses on general QA. We also have an ablation showing terminology-aware negative sampling matters. Help me structure the paper argument and decide what claims we can safely make.
evals.expected_output: A paper argument plan with thesis, claim hierarchy, section plan, figure/table plan, weak spots, and next actions.

evals.prompt: My draft has a method section and five experiment tables, but reviewers of an earlier version said the contribution was unclear. The method is basically contrastive pretraining with a curriculum. Can you find the argument spine and tell me what each section should prove?
evals.expected_output: A structured argument spine that narrows the contribution and maps sections and evidence to claims.

evals.prompt: I want to turn this workshop project into a full paper. We have one strong benchmark result, one failed transfer result, and some qualitative examples. What is the honest paper story?
evals.expected_output: A calibrated paper narrative that separates main, supporting, scope, and speculative claims.
